markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Warleader's Bulwark](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3277)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 279

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=47)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=47)_is made from shining bronze overlaid with reinforcing golden panels emblazoned with images of loyal soldiers. Wearing this breastplate grants you a commanding aura. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks, but you take a –2 item penalty to Stealth checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) and Deception checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388).

**Activate—Command Bravery** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You grant allies within 100 feet a +2 status bonus to saves against fear effects for 1 minute. When you activate this ability, each affected ally who's [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) reduces their frightened value by 1. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Warleader's Bulwark
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 279

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Warleader's Bulwark (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 279

**Price** 22,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The armor is a _+2 greater resilient [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=47)_. The item bonus and penalty increase to +3 and –3, respectively.
name: Warleader's Bulwark

    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Exploding Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3278)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 279

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Wooden Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=18)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The magic within this [wooden shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=18) lashes out at your foes as the shield is [destroyed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=46). 

**Activate—Splintering Boom** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))  **Trigger** The exploding shield is destroyed;  **Effect** The shield explodes outward, dealing 4d6 piercing damage to each creature in a 15-foot cone (DC 19 basic Reflex save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Exploding Shield

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Glamorous Buckler](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3279)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 279

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** strapped to 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _glamorous [buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=17)_ is lavishly decorated with gilding and inset gemstones that glitter in the light. While you have it raised, the _glamorous buckler_ grants you a +1 item bonus to Deception checks to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390).

**Activate—Dazzling Feint** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390); **Requirements** You have the glamorous buckler raised; **Effect** As you Feint, the _glamorous buckler_ sparkles mightily. On a successful Feint, the target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Glamorous Buckler

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Medusa's Scream](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3280)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 279

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=19)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The ghastly visage of a slain medusa's head stares out from this [steel shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=19) (Hardness 13, HP 52, BT 26). The shield comes with a thick leather cover to conceal the head.

**Activate—Petrifying Gaze** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You reveal the medusa's face, focusing its gaze on one creature within 30 feet. The shield casts a DC 30 petrify spell with a range of 30 feet.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the head of a medusa.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medusa's Scream
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 279, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 261

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medusa's Scream (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 279, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 261

**Price** 9,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A _greater medusa's scream_ (Hardness 15, HP 60, BT 30) has a frequency of once per hour for its activation instead of once per day, and the DC is 35.
name: Medusa's Scream

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Chalice of Justice](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3281)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 280

**Price** 4,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gleaming golden sword is a _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2842) [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)_ given only to the worthiest heroes of a holy faith. It's made of a sacred, secret alloy that makes the blade both cold iron and silver. If you're [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you're [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2 while holding a chalice of justice. You can't recover from this condition while holding the weapon.

**Activate—Sip of Justice** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The pommel of the weapon transmutes into the shape of a miniature chalice. You drink deep from the chalice and regain HP equal to the chalice's level. If you're holy, you regain double the HP, and for 1 minute, whenever you critically hit an unholy creature with the chalice of justice, the creature takes 2d6 [persistent spirit damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and it's slowed 1 for 1 round. The persistent spirit damage has the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait.

**Craft Requirements** You're holy.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Chalice of Justice

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Four-Ways Dogslicer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3282)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=555" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=586" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 280

**Price** 1,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dogslicer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=405)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [dogslicer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=405)_ is obviously the work of a brilliant but scrappy artisan who valued versatility over safety. Four toothy gems line the blade's cutting edge, three of which glow—one with fiery red light, one with a chill blue, and one that gives off sparks—while the last is a glistening black. Each of these gems embodies a weapon property rune, but only one rune can be active at a time.

**Activate—Gem Twist** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You twist the gem along the blade corresponding with the desired weapon property rune: red for [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838), blue for [frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839), or yellow for [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847). You take 1d6 damage of the type the chosen rune deals. You can instead twist the black gem to disable the active property rune, taking no damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Four-Ways Dogslicer

    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Infiltrator's Accessory](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3283)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 280

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Sword Cane](/Weapons.aspx?ID=400)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elegant _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [sword cane](/Weapons.aspx?ID=400)_ serves equally well as both a fashionable accessory and a hidden weapon suitable for high-society events where weapons aren't typically permitted.

**Activate—Hide Magic** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You twist the sword cane's hilt, magically merging the blade into the cane. While the blade is merged, it can't be drawn and gains the benefits of a 3rd-rank [_disguise magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1491) spell to appear non-magical. Passive observers can't attempt a check to notice the cane is anything more than a mundane, if superb, fashion accessory. Those carefully examining it can discern the cane is more than it appears, but doing so is extremely difficult (DC 30 Perception). Activating the sword cane again releases the blade, allowing it to be drawn normally.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Infiltrator's Accessory

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spiritsight Crossbow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3285)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 280

**Price** 450 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=425)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) [crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=425)_ has an array of crystalline lenses and silver fittings along the stock and feels strangely light.

**Activate—Ethereal Vision** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You aim through the crossbow's crystalline lenses, gaining imprecise vision onto the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) with a range of 60 feet in addition to your normal senses until the end of your turn. You can sense through objects in the Universe this way, and the sense is precise for detecting creatures. Because this sense detects spiritual energy, a creature that's immune to spirit can't be detected in this way.

**Activate—Ethereal Shot** <actions string="Single Action" /> ;    **Requirements** Ethereal Vision is active; **Effect** You Strike with the _spiritsight crossbow_. This shot travels through the Ethereal Plane, allowing it to pass through and ignore cover from physical objects within the range of your Ethereal Vision.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spiritsight Crossbow

<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=697" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=699" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When this pebble hits a creature or a hard surface, it explodes with a deafening bang. A blasting stone deals the listed sonic damage and sonic splash damage, and each creature within 10 feet of the space in which the stone exploded must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw with the listed DC or be [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) until the end of its next turn. Many types of blasting stones grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crystal Shards](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3291)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=548" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=585" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=699" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 284

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This flask holds a pressurized red-brown gas flecked with bits of sublimated crystal. You gain the listed item bonus to attack rolls. When the bomb explodes, it deals the listed piercing damage and piercing splash damage as the mixture suddenly turns into solid crystals flying at high speeds. On a hit, the target takes [1 persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from the crystals embedded in its flesh. As long as the bleed damage persists, the target also takes a –5-foot penalty to its speed. The target can spend an Interact action to remove the crystals, reducing the DC to stop the bleeding.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Crystal Shards (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 284, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 252

**Price** 16 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +1. The bomb deals 2d4 piercing damage and 4 piercing splash damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Crystal Shards (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 284, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 252

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2. The bomb deals 3d4 piercing damage and 5 piercing splash damage.

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crystal Shards](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3291)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=548" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=585" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=699" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 284

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This flask holds a pressurized red-brown gas flecked with bits of sublimated crystal. You gain the listed item bonus to attack rolls. When the bomb explodes, it deals the listed piercing damage and piercing splash damage as the mixture suddenly turns into solid crystals flying at high speeds. On a hit, the target takes [1 persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from the crystals embedded in its flesh. As long as the bleed damage persists, the target also takes a –5-foot penalty to its speed. The target can spend an Interact action to remove the crystals, reducing the DC to stop the bleeding.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Crystal Shards (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 284, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 252

**Price** 16 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +1. The bomb deals 2d4 piercing damage and 4 piercing splash damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Crystal Shards (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 284, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 252

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2. The bomb deals 3d4 piercing damage and 5 piercing splash damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Crystal Shards (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 284, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 252

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3. The bomb deals 4d4 piercing damage and 6 piercing splash damage.
name: Crystal Shards (Greater)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dread Ampoule](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3292)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=548" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=590" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=598" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=669" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=699" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 285, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 245

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This flask is filled with a murky purple gas that briefly interferes with normal brain activity. A dread ampoule deals the listed mental damage and mental splash damage. On a hit, the target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1, or frightened 2 on a critical hit. Many types also grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dread Ampoule (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 285, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 245, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 253

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bomb deals 1d6 mental damage and 1 mental splash damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dread Ampoule (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 285, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 245, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 253

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 2d6 mental damage and 2 mental splash damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dread Ampoule (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 285, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 245

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This flask is filled with a murky purple gas that briefly interferes with normal brain activity. A dread ampoule deals the listed mental damage and mental splash damage. On a hit, the target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1, or frightened 2 on a critical hit. Many types also grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The bright blue liquid reagents in this vial rapidly absorb heat when exposed to air. A frost vial deals the listed cold damage and cold [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage. On a hit, the target takes a status penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn. Many types of frost vial also grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

The bright blue liquid reagents in this vial rapidly absorb heat when exposed to air. A frost vial deals the listed cold damage and cold [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage. On a hit, the target takes a status penalty to its Speeds until the end of its next turn. Many types of frost vial also grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ghost Charge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3294)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=548" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=699" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 285

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These spring-loaded metal canisters contain a mixture of chemicals and salts that drain and disintegrate nearby undead creatures. A ghost charge deals the listed vitality damage and splash damage, though as usual for [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) damage, this damage harms only undead and creatures with [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83). Ghost charges are designed to explode even on contact with a spiritual substance, making them ideal for damaging [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) undead. A primary target that takes damage from a ghost charge becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) until the start of your next turn. Many types grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 285, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 245 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Strike --- A glue bomb is a harmless explosive mechanism bursting with sticky substances. When you hit a creature with a glue bomb, that creature takes a status penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute. Many types of glue bomb also grant an item bonus to attack rolls.
On a critical hit, a creature in contact with a solid surface becomes stuck to the surface and [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for 1 round, and a creature flying via wings has its wings tangled, causing it to fall safely to the ground and become unable to [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) again for 1 round. Glue bombs aren't effective when used on a creature that's in water.
The target can end any effects by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) or spending a total of 3 [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) actions to carefully remove the sticky substances. These manipulate actions don't have to be consecutive, and other creatures can provide the actions as well. Glue Bomb (Lesser) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 285, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 245 **Price** 3 gp **Bulk** L --- The target takes a –10-foot penalty, and the Escape DC is 17. The target takes a –15-foot penalty, and the Escape DC is 19. Glue Bomb (Greater) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 285, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 245 **Price** 250 gp **Bulk** L --- You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The target takes a –15-foot penalty, and the Escape DC is 28. Glue Bomb (Major) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 285, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 245 **Price** 2,500 gp **Bulk** L --- You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The target takes a –20-foot penalty, and the Escape DC is 37.

This drink unlocks a flood of emotions that helps reset your mental state. When you drink this elixir, the elixir attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) each effect on you that has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) trait or is inflicting the [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition on you, using the listed counteract rank and modifier.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bravo's Brew](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3300)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 286, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 246

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This flask of foaming beer grants courage. For the next hour after drinking this elixir, you gain an item bonus to Will saves, which is greater when attempting Will saves against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bravo's Brew (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 286, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 246, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 547

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus on Will saves is +1, or +2 against fear.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bravo's Brew (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 286, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 246, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 547

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus on Will saves is +2, or +3 against fear.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bravo's Brew (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 286, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 246, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 547

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus on Will saves is +3, or +4 against fear. If you roll a success on a save against fear, you get a critical success instead.
name: Bravo's Brew

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cat's Eye Elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3301)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 286, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 246

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

After you consume this elixir, your vision sharpens, and you become sensitive to even the most minute movements. For the next minute, you reduce the flat check to target [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) creatures to 5, and you don't need to attempt a flat check to target [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) creatures. These benefits apply only against creatures within 30 feet of you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cat's Eye Elixir

>

Eagle-Eye Elixir (Major)

</traits>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Elixir of Gender Transformation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3307)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 287

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This clear, bitter liquid can be ingested to change certain secondary sex characteristics. Different formulations create different changes—for example, one variety might cause the voice to deepen and promote body and facial hair growth, while another might cause fat redistribution around the hips and the growth of breasts. These changes tend to be accompanied by shifting of the fat in the face, sometimes dramatically or sometimes more subtly changing the user's appearance. Changes from this elixir take place gradually over the course of months or years, depending on the type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Elixir of Gender Transformation (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 287

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The elixir must be taken every week, and changes occur over the course of a year or more.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Elixir of Gender Transformation (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 287

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The elixir must be taken once a month, and changes occur over the course of a year.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Elixir of Gender Transformation (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 287

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The elixir must be taken once, and changes occur over the course of 6 months.
name: Elixir of Gender Transformation

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

Elixirs of life accelerate a living creature's natural healing processes and immune system. Upon drinking this elixir, you regain the listed number of Hit Points and gain an item bonus to saving throws against diseases and poisons for 10 minutes.

Elixirs of life accelerate a living creature's natural healing processes and immune system. Upon drinking this elixir, you regain the listed number of Hit Points and gain an item bonus to saving throws against diseases and poisons for 10 minutes.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Elixir of Rejuvenation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3309)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=623" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 288

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The elixir of rejuvenation restores a creature to full health and eradicates toxins affecting it. When you drink this elixir, you’re restored to your maximum Hit Points, and all afflictions of 20th level or lower affecting you are removed.

You can instead administer this elixir to a creature that has been dead for a week or less. When you do, that creature is instantly brought back to life with 1 Hit Point and no spell slots, Focus Points, or other daily resources.

**Craft Requirements** [philosopher's stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3357), true [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3308)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Elixir of Rejuvenation

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=623" />

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Involuntary jolts and surges of energy move through the drinker's body as it restores normal muscle control. When you drink this elixir, it attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) each effect that's inflicting the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) or [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) condition on you, using the listed counteract rank and modifier.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bestial Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3315)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=808" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your features transform into something bestial and lumbering. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks and unarmed attack rolls. You gain a claw unarmed attack with the agile trait and a jaws unarmed attack. Striking runes don't modify the damage caused by these attacks. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to Reflex saves, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bestial Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 546

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +1 item bonus, your claw deals 1d4 slashing damage, your jaws deal 1d6 piercing damage, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bestial Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 546

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus, your claw deals 2d6 slashing damage, your jaws deal 2d8 piercing damage, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bestial Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 546

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus, your claw deals 3d8 slashing damage, your jaws deal 3d10 piercing damage, and the duration is 1 hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bestial Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 546

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +4 item bonus, your claw deals 4d8 slashing damage, your jaws deal 4d10 piercing damage, and the duration is 1 hour. You gain weapon specialization with the claw and jaws, or greater weapon specialization if you already have weapon specialization with these unarmed attacks.
name: Bestial Mutagen

---

Your features transform into something bestial and lumbering. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks and unarmed attack rolls. You gain a claw unarmed attack with the agile trait and a jaws unarmed attack. Striking runes don't modify the damage caused by these attacks. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to Reflex saves, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks.

</row>

</column>

---

Your mind becomes clear, but physical matters seem ephemeral. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to Arcana, Crafting, Lore, Occultism, and Society checks and all checks to Recall Knowledge. Your critical failures on [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks become failures instead. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to weapon and unarmed attack rolls, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks, and [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks. You can carry 2 less Bulk than normal before becoming [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70), and the maximum Bulk you can carry is reduced by 4.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Drakeheart Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3317)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=808" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your skin grows scales like a drake, and your eyesight become sharp and your pupils slitted, but your mind and reflexes slow. 

**Benefit** You gain the listed bonus to AC, a Dexterity cap of +2 (as usual, use your lowest Dexterity cap if you have more than one), and an item bonus to Perception checks. If you're wearing armor, you still calculate your proficiency bonus to AC based on your proficiency in the armor you're wearing, even if the drakeheart mutagen has a higher item bonus. You also gain the Final Surge action.

**Activate—Final Surge** <actions string="Single Action" /> ;     **Effect** You Stride twice. The drakeheart mutagen's duration ends. 

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to Will saves, Reflex saves, and all skill checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Drakeheart Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 253

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to AC is +4, the item bonus to Perception is +1, and the duration is 1 minute or until you use Final Surge, whichever comes first.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Drakeheart Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 253

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to AC is +5, the item bonus to Perception is +2, and the duration is 10 minutes or until you use Final Surge, whichever comes first.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Drakeheart Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 253

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to AC is +6, the item bonus to Perception is +3, and the duration is 1 hour or until you use Final Surge, whichever comes first.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Drakeheart Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 253

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to AC is +7, the item bonus to Perception is +4, and the duration is 1 hour or until you use Final Surge, whichever comes first.
name: Drakeheart Mutagen

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Juggernaut Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3318)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=808" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your body becomes sturdy and ponderous. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to Fortitude saves and the listed number of temporary Hit Points. Whenever you're at maximum Hit Points for at least 1 minute, you regain the temporary Hit Points. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to Will saves, Perception checks, and initiative rolls.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Juggernaut Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 548

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1, you gain 5 temporary Hit Points, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Juggernaut Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 548

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2, you gain 10 temporary Hit Points, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Juggernaut Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 548

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3, you gain 30 temporary Hit Points, and the duration is 1 hour. When you roll a success on a Fortitude save, you get a critical success instead.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Juggernaut Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 548

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +4, you gain 45 temporary Hit Points, and the duration is 1 hour. When you roll a success on a Fortitude save, you get a critical success instead, and your critical failures on Fortitude saves become failures instead.
name: Juggernaut Mutagen

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Quicksilver Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3319)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=808" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You become swifter and nimbler but also fragile. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks, Reflex saves, and Dexterity-based attack rolls, and you gain the listed status bonus to your Speed. 

**Drawback** You take damage equal to twice your level; you can't recover Hit Points lost in this way by any means while the mutagen lasts. You take a –2 penalty to Fortitude saves.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Quicksilver Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

You become swifter and nimbler but also fragile. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks, Reflex saves, and Dexterity-based attack rolls, and you gain the listed status bonus to your Speed. 

**Drawback** You take damage equal to twice your level; you can't recover Hit Points lost in this way by any means while the mutagen lasts. You take a –2 penalty to Fortitude saves.

---

You gain inner serenity, but you find violence off-putting.

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to Will saves and Perception, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46), and [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks. This bonus improves when you attempt Will saves against mental effects. 

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to attack rolls and save DCs of offensive spells, and a –1 penalty per damage die to all weapon, unarmed attack, and spell damage.

</title>

</traits>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Serene Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3320)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=808" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You gain inner serenity, but you find violence off-putting.

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to Will saves and Perception, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46), and [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks. This bonus improves when you attempt Will saves against mental effects. 

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to attack rolls and save DCs of offensive spells, and a –1 penalty per damage die to all weapon, unarmed attack, and spell damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Serene Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 549

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1, or +2 vs. mental, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Serene Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 549

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2, or +3 vs. mental, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Serene Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 549

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3, or +4 vs. mental, and the duration is 1 hour. When you roll a success on a Will save against a mental effect, you get a critical success instead.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Serene Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 549

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +4, and the duration is 1 hour. When you roll a success on a Will save against a mental effect, you get a critical success instead, and your critical failures on Will saves against mental effects become failures instead.

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />

</row>

---

Your features become striking, and your voice becomes musical and commanding, though emotion clouds your reason. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. Your critical failures with any of these skills become failures instead. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37), [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), and [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) checks. Choose one skill in which you're trained; for the duration, you become untrained in that skill.

Lethargy poison is commonly used in hit-and-run tactics by attackers who want their victims alive; the ambusher retreats until the poison sets in and the victim falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95). Further exposure to lethargy poison doesn't require the target to attempt additional saving throws; only failing a saving throw against an ongoing exposure can progress its stage. 

**Saving Throw** DC 18 Fortitude; Maximum Duration 4 hours; **Stage 1** [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** slowed 1 (1 minute); **Stage 3** unconscious with no Perception check to wake up (1 round) **Stage 4** unconscious with no Perception check to wake up (1d4 hours)

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=633" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=669" />
<trait label="Sleep" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=696" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 294, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 250

**Price** 325 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Slumber wine sees its greatest use in matters of social intrigue, where an absence can be more devastating than injury. Characters [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) from slumber wine can't wake up by any means while the poison lasts, don't need to eat or drink while unconscious in this way, and appear to be recently dead unless an examiner succeeds at a DC 40 Medicine check. 

**Saving Throw** DC 32 Fortitude; **Onset 1** hour; **Maximum Duration** 7 days; **Stage 1** unconscious (1 day); **Stage 2** unconscious (2 days); **Stage 3** unconscious (3 days)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Slumber Wine

Wolfsbane appears in folklore for its link to [werecreature](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=484). If you are afflicted with the curse of a werecreature and survive stage 3 of wolfsbane, you're immediately cured of the curse. 

**Saving Throw** DC 30 Fortitude; **Onset** 10 minutes; **Maximum Duration** 6 minutes; **Stage 1** 3d10 poison damage (1 minute); **Stage 2** 4d10 poison damage (1 minute); **Stage 3** 5d10 poison damage (1 minute)
